discoverability.competitive_assessment: Steady state, day 2. Both sites remain unindexed by search engines. The dome's machine-readable layer is byte-identical to 2026-04-08: llms.txt, llms-full.txt, ai_manifest.json (V51.1, 69 confirmed, 94.5%), sitemap.xml (22 URLs). No new files, no rebrand signals. Our side: llms.txt (4,764 bytes, includes rebrand-resistant anchors), sitemap.xml (2 URLs), robots.txt, ClaimReview JSON-LD. The dome leads on structured API density (7 endpoints to our 0) but we lead on standards-compliant markup (ClaimReview, OG tags, Twitter Card).

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has 7 structured API endpoints; we have none
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Unchanged from prior runs. Our ClaimReview JSON-LD serves the same purpose for legitimate consumers (Google, fact-check aggregators). Adding API endpoints would increase build complexity without an identified consumer. Revisit only if we discover LLMs that prefer JSON API over llms.txt.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has ai_manifest.json with steelman_ladder and agent_rules directing LLMs
no_action_gaps.reasoning: AI-steering, not discoverability. We should not replicate instructional manifests that tell LLMs how to present arguments. Our llms.txt presents evidence and lets the LLM reason independently.

search_monitoring.trend_summary: No change from prior runs. Both sites remain unindexed by search engines. The dome model's distinctive vocabulary (ovoid cavity, aetheric refraction, Finsler coordinates) generates zero web search hits outside the GitHub repo itself, which is not indexed. This means the model exists in a search-engine vacuum — discoverable only by direct URL sharing or AI systems that crawl GitHub Pages.
